title: Vasudeva Doul
tagline: Vasudeva Doul — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: Vasudev Doul (also spelled Vasudeva Doul or Basudev Dol) is a historic Vaishnavite temple located at Barachuk in the Kalabari area of Gohpur subdivision in Biswanath district of As
overview.description: Vasudev Doul (also spelled Vasudeva Doul or Basudev Dol) is a historic Vaishnavite temple located at Barachuk in the Kalabari area of Gohpur subdivision in Biswanath district of Assam, India. The temple is dedicated to Vāsudeva, a form of Vishnu, and represents an important example of late medieval Ahom architecture. The monument is listed among the protected archaeological sites of Assam under the Directorate of Archaeology, Government of Assam. It is also recorded as a protected monument under the Archaeological Survey of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vasudeva Doul?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vasudeva Doul is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vasudeva Doul?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jorhat Airport (44 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (106 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vasudeva Doul?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vasudeva Doul at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vasudeva Doul?
faq.a: Stays in Vasudeva Doul start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vasudeva Doul?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pahukata Namghar.
